n0.hypothesis: Improve deliverable fit, adaptive concision, organization, claim scope, and evidence boundaries without weakening model fidelity
n0.insight: [from n1] Ordinary-task routing improved, but full-audit rendering must never infer absent scarcity, targets, constraints, or trade-offs and must suppress duplicate scaffolding

n1.hypothesis: Deliverable-first routing plus minimum-sufficient rendering will improve usability and profile compliance without sacrificing fidelity
n1.insight: Deliverable-first routing transfers on ordinary tasks, but full-audit generation still exposes excessive scaffolding and invents structure when semantics are absent

metadata.result: 8/8 adaptive contracts and 4/6 non-full wins passed, but full-audit simplification was 0/2 and XD-F01 added a material capacity trade-off
metadata.prune_reason: Frozen development merge gate rejected the combined candidate for material full-audit fidelity and no full-audit simplification

n2.hypothesis: Precise status, claim-granularity, contribution, and pathway rules will prevent scope and evidence overclaims
n2.insight: Explicit claim-scope rules help ordinary OR/IS outputs, but they do not prevent an inferred scarcity mechanism from entering a full audit

metadata.result: OR safeguards, IS mapping, and candidate scope/generalization gates passed; XD-F01 nevertheless introduced one material fidelity defect
metadata.prune_reason: Claim routing did not prevent a material inferred capacity trade-off in XD-F01

n3.hypothesis: Semantic single-home repetition control and adaptive table selection will improve organization without deleting high-value mappings
n3.insight: Single-home rules work in standard outputs but need a stronger full-audit render budget and compact-output final deletion pass

metadata.result: 5/8 outputs avoided repetition and standard mappings were useful, but OM-C01 and both full audits repeated substantive content
metadata.prune_reason: Semantic repetition remained in OM-C01 and both full audits
